title: LuhTech Skill Instance
description: Canonical schema for chat-side skill instances. Skills are the chat-behavior enforcement layer that pairs with rule instances (mechanical enforcement). JSON is canonical; SKILL.md is python-generated. Memory rule 19 (no-md-terminus) holds.

$schema.description: Pin to canonical schema URL. Refused on mismatch by instance-validation CI.

$id.description: URN form: urn:luhtech:skill:<category>:<skill-name>:<version>. Skill name is kebab-case, starts with lowercase letter.

name.description: Short identifier matching folder name. Kebab-case. Must match the last path segment of $id (excluding version).

description.description: Primary trigger string consumed by Claude's skill-routing layer. Combines what the skill does AND when to trigger. Per Anthropic skill-creator guidance: tend toward 'pushy' rather than passive to combat under-triggering.

version.description: Semver. Bump rules: major = description/triggerContext/behavior/pairedRule/compatibility changes; minor = description text refinement, lifecycleStatus UP-promotion; patch = examples, word-tuning, typo fixes.

category.description: Domain category. org=cross-cutting holdings-level; dev-pipeline=code/deliverable/schema work; comms=cross-chat/channel; governance=rule/schema lifecycle; infra=credentials/deploy/ops; venture=per-venture specialization.

lifecycleStatus.description: Maturity status. draft=authored, not in active use; working-model=loaded, fires on match; formalized=description optimized, evidence-backed; deprecated=superseded or retired.

triggerMode.description: Description-string posture. descriptive=neutral statement of purpose; pushy=actively encourages triggering (Anthropic-recommended default to combat under-triggering); eval-tuned=optimized via run_loop.py against held-out test set.

precondition.description: When the skill should fire — what state of the conversation triggers it.

exampleCues.description: Example user phrases or conversational cues that should fire this skill.

triggerContext.description: Structured array of preconditions describing when the skill fires. Generator renders this into SKILL.md trigger section.

summary.description: One-paragraph summary of what the skill does when triggered.

rationale.description: Why — tied to memory rule, rule instance, or failure mode being prevented.

directives.description: Ordered list of directives the skill enforces.

behavior.description: Structured body of skill behavior. Generator renders into SKILL.md instruction section.

author.description: Who authored this skill instance (free-form; matches deliverable + rule schema shape).

approver.description: Who approved this skill instance (typically Erik L4).

authority.description: Governance authority for the skill object itself. Matches authority block shape from deliverable.schema.json and rule.schema.json.

commitSha.description: Git commit SHA where the transition landed.

evidenceClass.description: Required on UP-promotions (to formalized). Cites the substrate that earned the promotion.

transitions.description: Audit trail of skill lifecycle transitions. UP-promotions to formalized require evidenceClass. Matches rule.schema.json transition shape.

pairedRule.description: Optional reference to a paired rule instance by $id. Lenient pairing — skills may exist without rule pairing. When populated, generator pulls rule cross-reference content into SKILL.md.

compatibility.description: Optional. Required tools, environment constraints, dependencies.

antiExample.description: Optional: what Claude should NOT do.

examples.description: Illustrative trigger scenarios. Renders into SKILL.md examples section.

boundaryConditions.description: Explicit cases where the skill should NOT fire — out-of-scope conditions.

location.description: Identifier, path, URI, or memory rule number.

references.description: Cross-references to memory rules, paired rule instances, supporting docs, bundled scripts, or assets.

generationMetadata.description: Populated by luhtech-schema skill generate. Records what generated the paired SKILL.md and from which JSON SHA. CI uses sourceJsonSha to detect drift.
